subject: fixing standardized robot
commit_message:
fixing standardized robot

-fixed communication via kube dns
-using fullname to avoid possible conflicts
-removed unused values
-set image to 1.2-STAGING-latest (for master)
-set demo artifacts to the master version 1.2.0-SNAPSHOT
-removed dependency on other reviews

Issue-ID: OOM-728

Change-Id: I60530215374149dd9d0e0706e37712ed8291ab6c
Signed-off-by: Mandeep Khinda <mandeep.khinda@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

Hi Mandeep,

As I am doing this in other component as well,

Just want to understand the conflicts here if we use the "name" instead of "fullname". As I am in favor of the prior as it will avoid double naming such as:-

robot-robot in pod/configmap and services.

Patch Set 1:

For sure. The short answer is: It is what you get when you run a "helm create foo".

In practice, I found that there were conflicts if say for example you wanted to deploy 2 separate robot chart releases in the same name space and just the chart name was used to create k8s objects.  Maybe this isn't a supported production use case but as a developer I might need to do this

I agree the resultant name doesn't look pretty but what does it matter?  It functions the same and adds flexibility.  

Also, it depends on what the user uses as their helm release name.  Simply just don't use "robot" as your release name.  When using the ONAP umbrella chart I am using something like "dev" to make this look better.

I have some other ideas that can add some smarts to the helm template that generates the name to make it look pretty.

Thank you for explanation and Yes, Agreed, if we could have use case like 2 chart in same namespace, then we need this and name prettiness doesnt matter. 
Anyways yes, will watch out and explore other ideas for same.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Ran robot health agains't a cherry picked AAI and SO.
Basic A&AI Health Check                                               | PASS |

SO gives an HTTP 503 but point is that they are communicating now :)
